channel.title: NYT > World > Americas
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: A Monument to Chocolate Is Wrapped in Layers of Mexican History
item.description: Combining a colonial house and a contemporary addition, the new Museum of Cacao & Chocolate sits on a grisly Aztec souvenir.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Gallow
item.media:credit: Luis Antonio Rojas for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Huei, or great Tzompantli. It displays hundreds of human skulls belonging to people who were believed to have been sacrificed in the 15th century.

item.title: U.S.-China Rivalry Reaches South American Skies
item.description: The U.S. has pressed Argentina and Chile to review two Chinese telescope projects in the Andean deserts. Astronomers are worried about setbacks to research.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola, Edward Wong and Sarah Pabst
item.media:description: The unfinished Chinese telescope at night at the Cesco observatory in El Leoncito, in Argentina’s San Juan Province.

item.title: After Capturing Maduro, Trump Celebrated Victory. Did It Bring Venezuelans Home?
item.description: Dire conditions in Venezuela prompted an exodus. In the wake of the attack that ousted its top leader, the question is whether things have changed enough to make a return appealing.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola, Patricia Sulbarán and Anita Pouchard Serra

item.title: Is This Telescope a Threat to U.S. Security?
item.description: Our reporter Emma Bubola visits the foothills of the Argentine Andes to find out why a giant Chinese-sponsored telescope is sitting dismembered.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola, Rebecca Suner, Leila Medina, Sutton Raphael, Sarah Pabst and Joey Sendaydiego

item.title: Passengers on Hantavirus Cruise Ship Will Disembark in Tenerife and Return to Home Countries
item.description: The cruise ship will arrive on the island of Tenerife, part of the Canary Islands of Spain, officials said. All of the passengers will then be evacuated to their home countries.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald and Carlos Barragán
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Workers on the island of Tenerife setting up tents at an expected reception point for passengers from the cruise ship MV Hondius.

item.title: Canada and the European Union Are in Love. Where Can It Lead?
item.description: A rapid trip with the prime minister to Armenia highlighted the new era of amity between Canada and the European Union. We examine what that can and can’t do for Canadians.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada, left; Mark Rutte, NATO’s secretary general; and Ukraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelensky, at the European Political Community summit in Yerevan. Armenia, on Monday.

item.title: Norovirus Outbreak Sickens 115 on Cruise Ship
item.description: More than 100 passengers and 13 crew members on the Caribbean Princess reported being ill, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: The Caribbean Princess left Port Everglades in Fort Lauderdale, Fla., on April 28 and is expected to return to Florida on Monday.

item.title: There Are Ants in This Canadian Hospital. Again.
item.description: A hospital in Manitoba had to postpone a “limited number of elective surgeries” after ants appeared there for the third time since 2024.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.media:description: The provincial authority that runs Carman Memorial Hospital in Carman, Manitoba, said it was working with patients to reschedule elective surgeries after ants appeared in the operating room.

item.title: Before Hantavirus Outbreak, the MV Hondius Attracted ‘a Different Type of Traveler’
item.description: Former passengers on the MV Hondius say that the ship was made for wildlife expeditions and that the crew took safety very seriously.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Claire Moses and Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:description: The MV Hondius off the port of Praia, Cape Verde, on Sunday. Passengers on the ship showed symptoms of the hantavirus, and some died.

item.title: Four Men Found Guilty of Haiti President’s Assassination
item.description: President Jovenel Moïse of Haiti was shot in his bedroom in July 2021, but the convictions for conspiracy have not revealed who ordered his assassination.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams and Frances Robles
item.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural of Jovenel Moïse near his home in Port-au-Prince, Haiti, where he was assassinated in 2021.

item.title: U.S. to Review Mexican Consulates After Right-Wing Claims Against Them
item.description: The State Department said it would review all Mexican consulates in the United States, following claims in conservative media that the consulates interfere in politics.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas
item.media:credit: Alisha Jucevic for The New York Times
item.media:description: The State Department is reviewing the 53 Mexican consulates in the U.S., including the consulate in Los Angeles.

item.title: Trump Hosts Brazil’s President Lula at White House After Months of Ups and Downs
item.description: President Trump and President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who have had a rocky relationship, met for talks on trade and other issues. But they skipped a planned joint appearance.
item.dc:creator: Adam B. Ellick
item.media:description: President Trump with President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva of Brazil at an economic forum last year in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia.

item.title: Watercraft Slams Into Gray Whale Near Vancouver, Injuring Driver
item.description: The whale appeared to be unharmed by the collision, which was captured on video.
item.dc:creator: John S.W. MacDonald

item.title: Kendall Myers, American Spy for Cuba, Dies at 88
item.description: While working for the State Department, he delivered top secret information to his Cuban handlers for 30 years. He was convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment.
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.media:description: Kendall Myers in 2009 before his arrest that year. “We did not intend to hurt any individual American,” he said before being sentenced. “Our only objective was to help the Cuban people defend their revolution.”

item.title: A Hotel in Venezuela’s Capital Has Become the U.S. Embassy’s De Facto Headquarters
item.description: The J.W. Marriott in Venezuela’s capital is suddenly awash in U.S. diplomats, spies and fortune seekers, showcasing changes in the country.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero and Todd Heisler
item.media:description: The Marriott’s top floor serves as the de facto headquarters of the U.S. Embassy in Caracas.

item.title: World Cup Dynamic Ticket Pricing Drives Argentine Soccer Fans To Extremes
item.description: Dynamic ticket pricing has made this year’s tournament the most expensive in history, pushing Argentine fans to extremes.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja, Emma Bubola, Lucía Cholakian Herrera and Sarah Pabst
item.media:description: River Plate soccer fans at Estadio Monumental stadium in Buenos Aires, at the biggest club game in Argentina against rival Boca Juniors last month.

item.title: Argentine Fans Are Struggling to Afford This World Cup
item.description: Argentine fans have flocked to previous World Cups by the tens of thousands, but the high cost of attending this year’s event is creating a new challenge. Tariq Panja, a global sports correspondent for The New York Times, traveled to Buenos Aires to meet fans who were reckoning with the rising prices, which FIFA said would create income to help fund soccer development around the world.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja, Leila Medina and Sarah Pabst

item.title: Reclaiming the Name of the Black Hero Who Inspired ‘Uncle Tom’s Cabin’
item.description: After 42 years of slavery, Josiah Henson escaped to Canada, where he wrote a memoir, founded a school and led others to freedom. But his home long bore the name “Uncle Tom,” to the offense of many.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen and Ian Willms
item.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Henson House at the Josiah Henson Museum of African-Canadian History in Dresden, Canada.

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreaks Are Rare, but They Aren’t Going Away and There’s No Cure
item.description: Since the family of rodent-borne infections were identified in the 1950s, they have turned up all over the world.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:description: A cruise ship remained stranded off the coast of Cape Verde after a suspected hantavirus outbreak on the vessel killed at least three people and left several others ill.

item.title: Louise Arbour Named as Canada’s Governor General
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s appointment of Louise Arbour, a former Supreme Court justice, as governor general, sends a message beyond Canada.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada with the country’s new governor general, Louise Arbour, in Ottawa on Tuesday.

item.title: Can Javier Milei’s Deregulatory Frenzy Buoy the Global Right?
item.description: With his signature chain saw, Javier Milei made himself a symbol of a global movement. At home, his state-slashing experiment is on the line.
item.dc:creator: Roger Cohen

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Boat in Caribbean, Killing 2
item.description: The aerial strike raised the death toll to at least 187 in the U.S. campaign against boats the Trump administration accuses of smuggling drugs.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.category: US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: An image from a video released by U.S. Southern Command showing a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel conducted by Joint Task Force Southern Spear.

item.title: U.S. Revokes Visas of Board Members at Costa Rica’s Top Watchdog Newspaper
item.description: The move came after the paper critically covered the country’s president, who has cozied up to the United States. Analysts view the visa decision as part of a White House strategy to punish critics of its allies.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega
item.media:credit: Mark Schiefelbein/Agence France-Presse, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, left, with President Rodrigo Chaves of Costa Rica last year.

item.title: Mark Carney Pulls Canada Closer to Europe as Both Struggle With Trump
item.description: At a summit of European leaders, the Canadian prime minister was a special guest offering deals and friendship to jittery allies.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada arriving for a meeting of the European Political Community on Monday in Yerevan, Armenia.

item.title: Monster Truck Drives Into Crowd in Colombia, Killing 3
item.description: The driver of the truck appeared to lose control of the vehicle after performing a stunt at an outdoor rally in Popayán.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.media:credit: Colombia’s National Police Press, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A monster truck after an accident during a show in Popayán, Colombia, on Sunday.

item.title: What to Know About the Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
item.description: Health authorities said three passengers from the MV Hondius had died after showing symptoms of the rare disease.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses and Jin Yu Young
item.media:description: The cruise ship MV Hondius stationed off the port of Praia, Cape Verde, on Sunday.
